source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A small object is placed 50 cm to the left of a thin convex lens of focal length 30 cm. A convex spherical mirror of radius of curvature 100 cm is placed to the right of the lens at a distance of 50 cm. The mirror is tilted such that the axis of the mirror is at an angle θ = 30° to the axis of the lens, as shown in the figure.

If the origin of the coordinate system is taken to be at the centre of the lens, the coordinates (in cm) of the point (x,y) at which the image is formed are
<image_1>
extraction_notes: answer field is option label 'C'; mapped to corresponding option text
image_1 contains an in-memory PIL image descriptor, not a path/URL, so image_paths is empty

issues: metadata.extraction_notes states image_1 was an in-memory PIL image descriptor rather than a path/URL
metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains 1 path

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
the tilted mirror geometry and final (x, y) image location are spatial/diagrammatic
an image asset exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the setup combines a convex lens and a convex spherical mirror, so solving likely requires chaining more than one optics step
the mirror axis is tilted by 30 degrees relative to the lens axis, adding a geometric transformation beyond a single formula lookup
the target is a coordinate pair (x, y), which implies deriving position from intermediate image formation rather than directly reading one scalar quantity
verifiability: answer_text is a specific exact coordinate pair: '(25, 25√3)'
the problem provides concrete numerical parameters (object distance, focal length, radius of curvature, separation, tilt angle)
multiple-choice options are given, enabling straightforward final-answer checking

risk_flags: figure referenced explicitly; some geometric conventions may depend on the image
metadata.extraction_notes states image_1 was an in-memory PIL image descriptor rather than a path/URL
metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains 1 path

decision_reasons: Question text, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantics are recoverable.
Initial scoring is strong: high image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability scores.
The figure is explicitly required, and a valid image asset exists with registry_passed=true.
Recorded issues are metadata inconsistencies rather than missing core assets, so they do not block candidacy.
